description.content: Operations for creating and managing listeners

item.name: Amazon Elastic Load Balancing Create a Listener

request.name: Amazon Elastic Load Balancing Create a Listener

description.content: Creates a listener for the specified Application Load Balancer, Network Load Balancer, or Gateway Load Balancer. Listeners check for connection requests from clients using the protocol and port that you configure.

query.description: (Required) The ARN of the load balancer
The protocol for connections from clients to the load balancer
(Required) The port on which the load balancer is listening
The security policy that defines which protocols and ciphers are supported
(Required) The type of action
The ARN of the target group for forward actions

request.name: Amazon Elastic Load Balancing Describe Listeners

description.content: Describes the specified listeners or the listeners for the specified Application Load Balancer, Network Load Balancer, or Gateway Load Balancer.

query.description: The ARN of the load balancer
The ARNs of the listeners
The ARNs of the listeners

The maximum number of results to return

Operations for creating and managing load balancers

item.name: Amazon Elastic Load Balancing Create a Load Balancer

request.name: Amazon Elastic Load Balancing Create a Load Balancer

description.content: Creates an Application Load Balancer, Network Load Balancer, or Gateway Load Balancer. For more information, see the User Guide for the specific load balancer type.

query.description: (Required) The name of the load balancer. Must be unique per region per account, can have a maximum of 32 characters.
The IDs of the subnets. You can specify only one subnet per Availability Zone.
The IDs of the subnets. You can specify only one subnet per Availability Zone.
The IDs of the security groups for the load balancer
The IDs of the security groups for the load balancer

The scheme for the load balancer. An internet-facing load balancer routes requests from clients to targets over the internet. An internal load balancer routes requests to targets using private IP addresses.
The type of load balancer
The type of IP addresses used by the subnets

Operations for creating and managing listener rules

item.name: Amazon Elastic Load Balancing Create a Listener Rule

request.name: Amazon Elastic Load Balancing Create a Listener Rule

description.content: Creates a rule for the specified listener. The listener must be associated with an Application Load Balancer. Rules are evaluated in priority order, from the lowest value to the highest value.

query.description: (Required) The ARN of the listener
(Required) The rule priority. Must be unique within the listener.
The name of the field (host-header, path-pattern, etc.)
The condition values
The condition values
(Required) The type of action
The ARN of the target group for forward actions

Operations for creating and managing target groups

item.name: Amazon Elastic Load Balancing Create a Target Group

request.name: Amazon Elastic Load Balancing Create a Target Group

description.content: Creates a target group. Target groups are used to route requests to one or more registered targets. After creating a target group, you can register targets and create listeners or rules to route traffic to the target group.

query.description: (Required) The name of the target group. Must be unique per region per account.
The protocol to use for routing traffic to the targets
The port on which the targets receive traffic
The identifier of the VPC for the targets
The protocol to use for health checks
The port to use for health checks
The destination for health checks on the targets
The type of target to register with the target group

Operations for registering and deregistering targets

item.name: Amazon Elastic Load Balancing Register Targets with a Target Group

request.name: Amazon Elastic Load Balancing Register Targets with a Target Group

description.content: Registers the specified targets with the specified target group. If the target is an EC2 instance, it must be in the running state when you register it.

query.description: (Required) The ARN of the target group
(Required) The ID of the target (instance ID, IP address, or Lambda ARN)
The port on which the target is listening
The Availability Zone where the target is located

item.name: Amazon Elastic Load Balancing Deregister Targets from a Target Group

request.name: Amazon Elastic Load Balancing Deregister Targets from a Target Group

description.content: Deregisters the specified targets from the specified target group. After the targets are deregistered, they no longer receive traffic from the load balancer.

query.description: (Required) The ARN of the target group
(Required) The ID of the target
The port on which the target is listening

AWS Region

info.name: Amazon Elastic Load Balancing v2 API

description.content:
Amazon Elastic Load Balancing automatically distributes incoming application traffic across multiple targets, such as Amazon EC2 instances, containers, IP addresses, and Lambda functions. It supports Application Load Balancers, Network Load Balancers, and Gateway Load Balancers.

Contact Support:
 Name: Amazon Web Services
